title: Pariyanempatta Bhagavathi Temple
tagline: Pariyanempatta Bhagavathi Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: The Pariyanempatta Bhagavathi Temple is one of the famous temples of Kerala, India, dedicated to Bhagavathy.
overview.description: The Pariyanempatta Bhagavathi Temple is one of the famous temples of Kerala, India, dedicated to Bhagavathy. This is one of the largest Devi temples of Valluvanad Desam in Palghat district. The presiding deity is known as the Goddess of 14 Desams. The temple and its precincts resemble that of Mookambika temple Kollur.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Sreekrishnapuram, Palakkad
topPlaces.description: Sreekrishnapuram is a town within the Ottappalam Tehsil of Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Karimpuzha Sree Ramaswamy Temple
topPlaces.description: Karimpuzha Sree Ramaswamy Temple (also known as the Dakshina Ayodhya) is located in Palakkad district, Kerala, India. It lies on the bank of the Karimpuzha River, a major tributary of the Bharathapuzha River.

topPlaces.name: Karimpuzha, Palakkad
topPlaces.description: Karimpuzha is a gram panchayat in the Palakkad district, in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Ambalappara
topPlaces.description: Ambalapara is a gram panchayat in the Palakkad district, state of Kerala, India. It is the local government organisation that serves the villages of Ambalapara-I and Ambalapara-II and forms a part of the Ottapalam taluk.

topPlaces.name: Vayilyamkunnu Bhagavathi Temple
topPlaces.description: Vayillyamkunnu Bhagavathi Temple is located at Kadampazhipuram, the historical land of Valluvanad Kerala, 24 km (15 mi) away from Palakkad on the way to Cherpulassery.

topPlaces.name: Vellinezhi
topPlaces.description: Vellinezhi is a village located in the Palakkad district of Kerala in southern India.

topPlaces.name: Ananganadi
topPlaces.description: Ananganadi is a village in Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India. Ananganadi is located equidistant from the towns of Ottapalam to the south and Cherpulassery to its north, both about 10 km from Ananganadi.

topPlaces.name: Cherpulassery
topPlaces.description: Cherpulassery (also known by its former name Cherpulacherry) is a town and municipality in the Palakkad district, of Kerala, India. Cherpulassery is often called the Sabarimala of Malabar as the famous Ayyappankavu temple is located here .

items.note: Sreekrishnapuram is a town within the Ottappalam Tehsil of Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India.

Karimpuzha Sree Ramaswamy Temple (also known as the Dakshina Ayodhya) is located in Palakkad district, Kerala, India. It lies on the bank of the Karimpuzha River, a major tributary of the Bharathapuzha River.
Karimpuzha is a gram panchayat in the Palakkad district, in the state of Kerala, India.

Vayillyamkunnu Bhagavathi Temple is located at Kadampazhipuram, the historical land of Valluvanad Kerala, 24 km (15 mi) away from Palakkad on the way to Cherpulassery.
Vellinezhi is a village located in the Palakkad district of Kerala in southern India.

Ananganadi is a village in Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India. Ananganadi is located equidistant from the towns of Ottapalam to the south and Cherpulassery to its north, both about 10 km from Ananganadi.

Cherpulassery (also known by its former name Cherpulacherry) is a town and municipality in the Palakkad district, of Kerala, India. Cherpulassery is often called the Sabarimala of Malabar as the famous Ayyappankavu temple is located here .

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pariyanempatta Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pariyanempatta Bhagavathi Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pariyanempatta Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (57 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (63 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pariyanempatta Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pariyanempatta Bhagavathi Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pariyanempatta Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Pariyanempatta Bhagavathi Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pariyanempatta Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sreekrishnapuram, Palakkad, Karimpuzha Sree Ramaswamy Temple, Karimpuzha, Palakkad, Ambalappara, Vayilyamkunnu Bhagavathi Temple.
